steps.title: Identify what the researchers' explanation requires
steps.content: The researchers explain that rearranging a store's layout causes shoppers to search through more of the store, exposing them to more products and increasing unplanned purchases. A supporting survey response must show this exact chain: relocated item $\Rightarrow$ extra wandering through the store $\Rightarrow$ spontaneous purchase.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• "I needed to buy some cleaning supplies, but they weren't in their regular place. While I was looking for them, I saw this interesting notebook and decided to buy it, too.": The shopper searched for a relocated item, wandered through more of the store, and made an unplanned purchase — exactly what the researchers describe. ✓
  • "I didn't buy everything on my shopping list today. I couldn't find a couple of the items in the store, even though I looked all over for them.": The shopper searched but made no unplanned purchase — no spontaneous buy occurs, so this does not support the explanation. ✗
  • "The store sent me a coupon for a new brand of soup, so I came here to find out what kinds of soup that brand offers. I decided to buy a few cans because I had the coupon.": The purchase is driven by a coupon, an external cue the text explicitly contrasts with layout rearrangement. ✗
  • "This store is larger than one that's closer to where I live, and it carries more products. I came here to buy some things that the other store doesn't always have.": The shopper chose this store for its stock, not because of layout changes; no unplanned purchase is described. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
